segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the group 'MAYYAS', dressed in matching black crop tops, long skirts, wigs, and ornate, bejeweled face veils, entering a brightly lit, futuristic stage with a star-themed backdrop. A female judge greets them with a clear "Good evening," and then asks, "Who are you?" A performer from MAYYAS, speaking with a confident voice, responds, "We are the Mayyas. It's a word in Arabic that means the proud walk of a lioness," further explaining that "Being a dancer as a female Arab... is not fully supported yet." When asked by the judge what they plan to do, the performer confidently states, "We're going to try to hypnotize you," eliciting an audible "Oh!" from a male judge and immediate audience cheers. Ethereal, suspenseful music with bell-like sounds and strings begins to build as the performance starts. A striking visual illusion unfolds: a central performer stands still while multiple arms emerge from behind her, creating the appearance of a multi-armed figure. The arms move in synchronized, fluid motions, extending and folding, enhanced by dynamic blue and purple lighting on the reflective floor. The judges, including the bald man in sunglasses and the woman in the sparkly top, watch with visible awe and surprise, while the audience gasps and cheers loudly. The illusion is maintained throughout, with intricate gestures culminating in a powerful, unified pose with arms raised and hands clasped, all set to dramatic, building orchestral music and continuous, enthusiastic audience applause.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the judging panel—comprising the bald man in sunglasses, Simon, Heidi, and the woman in sparkly top—reacting with enthusiastic applause and visible excitement, their hands raised in celebration, amidst loud crowd cheering and exclamations of "Wow!" I'm like freaking out right now." The scene shifts to a wide shot of the stage, revealing the full group ZURCAROH, dressed in earth-toned, tribal-inspired costumes, standing in a celebratory pose under bright lights, raising their arms in triumph, while the crowd continues to cheer. The camera returns to the judges, with Heidi raising her hands in awe and Simon clapping with a wide grin. Simon then addresses Mel B, who, identified as the woman in the sparkly top, expresses profound amazement, stating, "But all of you to be that in sync, I'm just I yeah, wow too... On point. So good." Heidi, also addressed by Simon, describes the performance as "unbelievable," detailing "kids flying all over the air" and expressing disbelief at how they were caught, even mentioning one performer was "in Simon's lap for a second," calling it "absolutely incredible." The young girl in the audience is shown again, still smiling and clapping. A close-up of ZURCAROH shows the central male performer speaking into the microphone, surrounded by his beaming teammates. The segment concludes with the group on stage, arms raised, as the audience and judges continue to cheer, capturing a moment of shared triumph and emotional connection, with on-screen text '@Zurcaroh #AGTpremiere' appearing in the bottom right corner.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage comes alive with vibrant blue and purple lighting as the audience erupts in cheers and applause. The judging panel, featuring Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie, is seated at their desk, each with a red Sonic-branded cup. The focus shifts to URBANCREW, a group of male dancers in bright red tracksuits, bandanas, and white sneakers, standing confidently in formation. Simon's voice cuts through the excitement, asking, "What do you do?" A lead member of URBANCREW, speaking with a distinct Filipino accent, explains they are "dancing and break dancing and acrobat," prompting Howie to exclaim, "All right!" amidst more audience cheers. Simon then inquires, "And why AGT?" The URBANCREW member passionately responds that it's "the biggest stage" and they believe "being a Filipino, it could be a big contribution for our beloved countries," emphatically adding, "Philippines!" to a wave of audience applause. Simon presses further, asking if they think they can win, to which the dancer confidently replies, "Yes, we do because we work hard for this," emphasizing it's a "once in a lifetime opportunity." Sofia, in her sparkly green top, playfully asks if they have "more fun now that you're blonde?" The URBANCREW member enthusiastically replies, "Yes, ma'am!" and Sofia exclaims, "I like it!" Simon observes, "I can tell they just want to perform," with Sofia adding, "They're ready. You're itching for this." After a "Yes, sir" from the performer, Simon encourages, "So let's do it. Good luck," and the URBANCREW member thanks him as the audience's cheers and applause swell, concluding the segment with the group standing proudly on stage.

Upbeat, high-energy electronic music pulses as the audience erupts in cheers and applause, with a male voice exclaiming, "This is incredible!" The group performs dynamic formations, flips, and lifts, culminating in a celebratory pose as golden confetti rains down. The crowd's excitement intensifies with shouts of "Yes!" and "Amazing!" from unseen voices, while the judges, including Sofia in a sparkly green top, react with visible excitement, clapping and exclaiming. Waffle Crew, a group of male performers in basketball-style jerseys with 'WAFFLE NEW YORK CITY' printed on them, stands in formation on stage. One member, holding a microphone, addresses the judges, including Simon, Heidi, Howie, and Sofia, who are seated at the judging panel with red Sonic-branded cups. The Waffle Crew member introduces themselves, stating, "We are Waffle Crew. We go by We Are Family For Life Entertainment, and we are a crew all the way from New York City, the Bronx, New York." Simon then asks, "How many why this year AGT? What's the dream and what's the plan?" The Waffle Crew member passionately explains their motivation: "We're from a city where there's no hope... We don't see the right things when we go back home... We never gave up on our hopes and dreams. So we want to give the young people something positive to look into." He continues, "We already invest in our community with just as little as our pocket money. So a million dollars right now, we would really, really put on for our city." Simon responds with "Good for you," as Sofia gestures animatedly, exclaiming "That was so high." and "What is going on?", and the audience continues to cheer and clap, maintaining a vibrant atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Waffle Crew, a group of male dancers in 'WAFFLE NEW YORK CITY' basketball-style jerseys, performing high-energy acrobatics on the America's Got Talent stage. The judging panel, including Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie, watches intently amidst building audience cheers and applause. Simon then speaks calmly and deliberately, stating, "I also understand how important... how important this is to you," followed by a surge of audience excitement. He continues, "I want to give you a little head start here," before pressing the Golden Buzzer. This triggers a massive explosion of golden confetti and pyrotechnics, accompanied by a dramatic, emotional song featuring a female vocalist singing "You know me when you're not strong... I'll be your friend... I'll be your guide..." The stage is bathed in warm, golden light as the Waffle Crew celebrates their victory with raised arms. Sofia gasps with her mouth wide open, exclaiming excitedly, "That was so high!" and "What is going on?!" The judges stand and applaud, while Simon addresses the group with a final "You..." The atmosphere shifts to jubilant celebration, with the America's Got Talent logo prominently displayed, and on-screen text '@WaffleNYC #AGT' and the NBC logo visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a celebratory moment on the America's Got Talent stage, as golden confetti rains down on the Waffle Crew, a group of male performers in basketball-style jerseys, who are being congratulated after receiving the Golden Buzzer. An excited announcer exclaims, "They've received the Golden Buzzer!" The group, dressed in matching gray and white jerseys with black shorts, embraces each other and the judges amidst the cheers and applause of the thrilled audience. Simon, in a white shirt, is heard telling them, "Guys, you're incredible! That was fantastic! That's a moment this young man will never, ever forget." He and Howie, in a pink suit, hug and congratulate the performers, while Sofia, in a sparkly green top, and Heidi, in a purple dress, react with excitement under the warm golden lighting. An interviewer then asks Simon, "Simon, what made you push this Golden Buzzer for these amazing young men?" to which Simon responds, "Talent, determination, and everything I have been waiting for on the show, and they've delivered it." He adds, "Welcome to America's Got Talent. This is the biggest stage in the world. This is your time now." The scene transitions to a recap segment for the group Junior New System, who are dressed in black jackets with red and white stripes and matching black shorts, standing in formation on a stage lit with cool blue and purple lights, featuring a cityscape backdrop with palm trees. The audience cheers as one member, holding a microphone, thanks them, "Thank you!" Simon then asks, "What's your name, please?" and the member replies, "We are the Junior New System from Manila, Philippines." Simon continues, "How long have you been a group?" and the member states, "We've been dancing for nine years and we're all members." The atmosphere shifts as the member emotionally reveals, "In the Philippines, we're... we are street children," his voice breaking with sniffles. Judges, including Mel B in a sparkly top, react with amazement, with a female voice (likely Mel B) commenting, "He's crying. Oh. These are happy tears, yes? Because this is..." as Mel B raises her hands in excitement. On-screen text displays '7 JUNIOR NEW SYSTEM TALENT RECAP' and 'WORLD OF DANCE PREMIERES NEXT', along with the NBC logo in the bottom left corner, highlighting the contrast between the two performances and the judges' reactions.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage erupts with high-energy dance music and roaring audience cheers as Junior New System, dressed in matching black jackets with red and white stripes and black shorts, performs a dynamic, synchronized routine featuring acrobatic lifts and sharp choreography. The atmosphere is electric, with the audience cheering loudly throughout. As the group culminates their powerful formation, the crowd gives a standing ovation, and the judges—Simon, Heidi, Mel B, and Howie—react with visible excitement, all pressing their buzzers and rising from their seats. Mel B exclaims, "What just happened?!" in disbelief, while a male voice shouts, "Yeah bro!" The performers celebrate on stage, hugging and raising their arms in triumph as golden confetti rains down, with one dancer wiping tears. A member of Junior New System repeatedly expresses gratitude, saying, "Thank you so much!" Mel B continues her praise, highlighting "The heels, the flipping, the choreography, I wasn't expecting it. You've come all the way from the Philippines. I love you!" Howie then adds, "This was amazing. Before you even started, the emotion that you had and showing us your heart, telling us what this meant to you. You are the epitome of dreams coming true." Simon begins to offer his feedback, stating, "I think that when you're judging something like this, I always like to judge it on an entertainment basis. I thought the first half of the act..." before his comment is cut off. The judges' table, adorned with red Sonic-branded cups, is shown as they applaud enthusiastically, with Mel B throwing her hands up in amazement. The stage backdrop features the iconic America's Got Talent logo, and the on-screen text '@JNSOfficial #AGTpremiere' appears, capturing the overwhelming approval and celebration.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the Junior New System, a group of young male performers in black jackets with red and white stripes, seated and reacting with anticipation. A male judge, wearing a white shirt and seated at the judging panel with a Dunkin' Donuts cup, speaks into a microphone with expressive hand gestures, telling a previous act, "I've kind of seen it before. Then the second half, the heels come on, the personality comes through, and you just blown this audience away. Good for you." The audience cheers as he announces, "You guys have got four yeses. Congratulations!" A performer, presumably from Junior New System, responds with an enthusiastic "Thank you so much, sir! Thank you so much!" as the audience erupts in applause and celebratory music begins. The camera then pans to a wide shot of the America's Got Talent stage, featuring a large, illuminated star backdrop and a semi-circular, geometric prop. The group Diavolo, dressed in all-white, form-fitting outfits, runs onto the stage and lines up. Their leader, holding a microphone, introduces them to the judge as "Diavolo, architecture in motion," explaining they are "a movement company based here in Los Angeles" that mixes "a lot of different movement types together with beautiful structures." The judge, initially confused, asks "Okay, I still don't know what that means. Who owns this?" to which the leader replies, "Our founder, his name is Jacques Heim. He's the creative director and the founder of Diavolo." A red banner displays '8 DIAVOLO TALENT RECAP' and their origin 'Los Angeles, CA'. Dramatic, orchestral music with a female vocalization begins as the performance starts. Two Diavolo dancers execute a dynamic acrobatic routine on the semi-circular prop, with one balancing atop the other in a handstand, then transitioning into a synchronized lift. The stage is bathed in blue and purple lighting, with the America's Got Talent logo prominently displayed. The audience is visible, cheering and clapping as the performance continues with fluid, gravity-defying movements, including a dancer flipping over the prop while another holds a stable pose. The camera captures the routine from multiple angles, emphasizing the precision and strength of the performers. The segment ends with the group standing together, smiling, as the audience applauds to the fading dramatic music.

Ethereal, slightly ominous music with a deep bass and high-pitched synth notes begins to build as the group Diavolo, dressed in all-white, form-fitting outfits, runs onto the stage and assembles around a large, illuminated semi-circular geometric prop with a lattice design. A female voice exclaims, "That no is off!", followed by another female voice, likely the woman in sparkly top, saying, "It's gonna fall over," as the performers execute a synchronized acrobatic routine, using the prop for lifts, balances, and dynamic movements. The music intensifies with percussive elements as one performer is lifted high into the air, performing a handstand on the edge of the structure, while others support and maneuver around it. The routine includes dramatic flips, rolls, and coordinated group formations, with performers moving fluidly in and out of the prop. A male voice, likely the man in white shirt, exclaims "Wow!" as the audience reacts with visible excitement, and the judges display expressions of astonishment. The woman in sparkly top is seen with her mouth agape, while the man in white shirt, with a Dunkin' Donuts cup, watches intently. As the music becomes more energetic and rhythmic, the woman in sparkly top shouts "Oh no!" with concern, and another male voice exclaims "Whoa!". The woman in sparkly top then cries "Oh my God!" in a mix of shock and excitement. The performance culminates in a final, powerful pose with performers striking dynamic positions around the prop, accompanied by a powerful musical crescendo. The judges, including the bald man in sunglasses who shouts "Whoa!" with amazement, stand to applaud enthusiastically, joined by the audible cheers and applause from the audience. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the audience, visibly excited and clapping, their cheers and applause blending with intense, dramatic instrumental music. On the America's Got Talent stage, the group Diavolo, dressed in all-white form-fitting outfits, performs a high-energy acrobatic routine on a large, illuminated semi-circular geometric prop. The performers execute synchronized flips, lifts, and dynamic movements, including a dramatic mid-air suspension, all set against vibrant blue and purple lighting. The judges—Simon, Heidi, Mel B, and Howie—react with astonishment, their mouths agape and eyes wide, as the music builds to a crescendo. The audience erupts in roaring cheers and applause, punctuated by a distinct 'BZZZZZZZ' sound as all four judges press their buzzers in unison, standing to applaud. Simon, with a clear male voice, remarks, "I kind of feel we need to bring Jacques out." Mel B, with an excited female voice, exclaims, "There's somebody out there. Go, go, go, go, go!" Howie, with a surprised male voice, adds, "Uh-oh!", followed by Heidi's excited female affirmation, "Yeah!" The performers conclude with a powerful final pose amidst continued audience 'WOOOO!'s and applause. Howie then states his own name, "Howie," before reflecting on judging, saying, "You know, when I see people dance or do acrobatics, I constantly say as a judge, I want you to take it to the next level." On-screen text '@Diavolo #AGT' and '8 DIAVOLO TALENT RECAP' identifies the group and segment, while the NBC logo, judges' red Sonic-branded cups, and Simon's Dunkin' Donuts cup are visible throughout the electric atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens on the America's Got Talent stage, illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lighting, where the group Diavolo, dressed in matching white, form-fitting outfits, stands in formation behind a central figure holding a microphone. Judge Howie's voice is heard praising them, stating, "And you guys took it beyond the next level. I have never seen anything quite like this in my life," as the camera cuts to the judging panel. The audience erupts in cheers and applause. The central performer, a man with long hair and a black shirt, responds with "Thank you," and explains, "it's those dancers that actually create the pieces, which is a beautiful thing." The audience cheers again. Heidi, in a purple dress, then shares her amazement, describing the act as "something different," "dangerous looking," and praising the "timing," noting she thought "someone is getting squashed under this thing" and "people are flying off of it," concluding, "it was incredible. Wow!" The audience cheers and applauds once more, as the central performer again says "Thank you" multiple times. Mel B, in a black lace outfit, expresses her excitement, finding it "so exciting" but "too short," wishing it "needed to be longer, much, much, much longer," and wanting to "see it again and again and again." The audience cheers. Simon, wearing a dark shirt, then praises the act as "really, really clever" and "something I will remember," calling it "incredible" and offering "congratulations." As the audience cheers and applauds with swelling music, the judges stand and applaud enthusiastically, with Simon pressing his buzzer and raising his hand. Howie is heard asking, "Okay, right, Howie, yes or no?" to which Simon enthusiastically replies, "Oh, yes!" The segment concludes with Diavolo bowing, the judges continuing to applaud, and the America's Got Talent logo displayed above the stage, with on-screen text '8 DIAVOLO TALENT RECAP' and '@Diavolo #AGT' visible, along with the NBC logo.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with FUSION JAPAN, a group of adults and children in traditional red and black attire, executing a high-energy, synchronized routine on the America's Got Talent stage, illuminated by vibrant blue and purple lighting with a prominent star backdrop. The audience is audibly cheering and clapping enthusiastically, with Simon exclaiming "in my life!" as the performance concludes. The group stands in formation, smiling and bowing to thunderous applause and an upbeat, dramatic musical score. The judging panel reacts with amazement; Sofia, in a sparkly top, is seen with her mouth agape, then declares, "Fusion Japan! That was amazing!" A male performer from FUSION JAPAN responds with "Thank you. Arigato gozaimasu yo." Mel B adds, "I've never seen anyone fall on their own face like that," followed by laughter, then praises, "It was spectacular, it was so powerful." Another FUSION JAPAN member is heard speaking briefly in Japanese. Heidi then states, "One of the best dance groups I have ever seen on America's Got Talent." As the audience continues to cheer, Howie asserts, "This is an act that I'm not gonna forget. And people are gonna want to share this and talk about it." Simon chimes in, "That was a Vegas show right there," and later elaborates, "It is quite incredible. We've seen a lot coming on this stage from all over the world and showing us something we've never seen before, which is really unique. And this was a great example of that." All four judges—Simon, Heidi, Howie, and Mel B—stand to applaud, with Simon and Howie raising their hands in excitement. Close-ups show a young boy in a red outfit beaming with pride, while the on-screen text '@FusionJapan #AGT' appears, signifying a Golden Buzzer moment amidst the celebratory atmosphere. Sofia is heard briefly at the end, saying "Okay, so we'll..." as the segment concludes with continued audience cheers and music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a vibrant, neon-lit performance by neon-costumed performers, dressed in black outfits outlined with glowing blue, green, and red lights, star-adorned hats, and red shoes, executing a synchronized dance routine on a dark stage. Upbeat electronic dance music with a strong beat accompanies their dynamic choreography against a neon cityscape backdrop featuring glowing skyscrapers, a bridge, and basketball hoops with 'GAME ON' and 'DUNK' signs. A central performer, wearing a red jersey with 'LIGHT' and '21', executes a basketball dunk. The scene transitions to a live performance on the America's Got Talent stage, where the same group, now in black and yellow costumes with glowing yellow joints, performs to roaring crowd cheers and applause. The judging panel, including a woman in yellow top, a woman in sparkly top, a bald man in sunglasses, and a man in white shirt, reacts with excitement, clapping, and pressing their buzzers, with one male judge exclaiming "Wow!" and a female judge adding "I want more!". The audience is visibly engaged, cheering, screaming, and raising their hands. A female voice announces, "Light Balance Kids tore the house down! Julianne, let's start with you." The woman in yellow top, identified as Julianne, then praises the act, stating, "Oh my goodness. Well, first of all, this round is all about stepping it up. You did not disappoint. The creativity, the innovation..." The performance concludes with the group bowing as golden confetti rains down, and the judges stand to applaud, all while the on-screen text '@Light_Balance #AGT' is displayed.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a young girl from LIGHT BALANCE KIDS, dressed in a black and yellow robotic costume, smiling confidently on the America's Got Talent stage, which is illuminated with vibrant purple and gold lighting and features a large star backdrop. Her voice is heard exclaiming, "was off the charts. Thank you. So good," amidst enthusiastic audience cheers. The camera then cuts to the judging panel, where Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie are seated behind a transparent desk with red Sonic-branded cups. Howie, smiling and leaning forward, praises the group, stating he was "a fan of Light Balance, the adults," but "even like Light Balance Kids better than the regular Light Balance." He continues, expressing, "I would go pay to see this in Vegas. I would see this at a live concert. I love you guys," as Sofia and Heidi clap excitedly. The young girl from LIGHT BALANCE KIDS responds with a grateful "Thank you." Simon then offers his praise, remarking, "I haven't seen people your age, so young, do something so brilliant, so unique, and the level of rehearsal that must go into that is quite incredible. I think you could be stars." The group again thanks him. Sofia, introducing herself as a guest judge, says, "I'm new here, I'm just a guest," before describing the performance as "the definition of a spectacle," "truly spectacular," and "marvelous." She then expresses a wish for their advancement, noting, "there are only seven acts that can go forward, and I just wish there were some guarantee to get you guys through to the live show." Throughout these comments, the camera returns to the six young performers in their matching robotic outfits, standing in formation. All four judges, including Simon, Sofia, Heidi, and Howie, are seen reacting with increasing enthusiasm, clapping, standing, and pressing their buzzers, while the audience cheers and raises their hands. The stage is filled with dynamic lighting, and the on-screen text '@Light_Balance #AGT' appears, highlighting the celebratory atmosphere as the performers beam with joy.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of the audience, where a young girl in a gray outfit claps excitedly, a woman in a black top covers her mouth in amazement, and a man in a black shirt gestures animatedly, all while enthusiastic audience cheering and clapping erupts, punctuated by a male voice (likely a judge) exclaiming, "Such hard work, I can't imagine," and "Fantastic, thank you." The camera pulls back to a wider audience shot, showing a woman in a yellow top and a woman in a white jacket reacting with visible excitement, as a man in a black shirt and a woman in a sparkly top stand and applaud, with the male voice adding, "You're in the live shows!" A dramatic musical sting then signals a transition to the America's Got Talent stage. The group 'JUST JERK', dressed in white satin outfits with black sashes and headbands, walks onto the stage to more audience applause. Standing in formation on the red star, their central performer, a woman holding a microphone, addresses the judges. The man in a white shirt (a judge) asks, "What is the name of your group?" to which the central performer responds, "We are Just Jerk crew from South Korea." He then asks, "And why did you decide to come on AGT?" The central performer explains, "Well, um, it might be our last big performance together as a crew. So, we thought AGT is the biggest stage for us." When asked, "Why would this be the last time?" she reveals, "In South Korea, all male, we have to serve in the military for two years... But after we're done with the service, we really don't know what's gonna happen. So, this is our big shot." The judge then inquires, "And what kind of dance is it?" and she confidently replies, "Something that you guys have never seen before," prompting a female judge to exclaim "Woo!" amidst renewed audience cheers, before the man in a white shirt concludes, "Alright." The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue and purple lighting, featuring a neon cityscape backdrop, while on-screen text displays '12 JUST JERK TALENT RECAP' and '@JustJerk #AGT'.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the group JUST JERK, dressed in matching white satin outfits with black sashes and headbands, standing in formation on the America's Got Talent stage. The judge in the white shirt, Simon Cowell, is heard saying, "remember, you did great guys," followed by applause and cheers. The central performer, holding a microphone, responds with "Thank you." Simon then asks, "What is Korean for yes?" to which the central performer replies, "You can say 'choa'." Simon repeats "Choa," then declares, "Okay, I'm gonna say choa. You got three choas." With expressive hand gestures, Simon adds, "And from me, you have your fourth!" The group, now seen from behind with '@JustJerkCrew #AGT' on-screen, receives this with the central performer saying, "Thank you so much." A transition sound leads into a dynamic 'FAN QUESTIONS' graphic, accompanied by dramatic then upbeat music. A woman with red braided hair and sunglasses on her head appears, introducing herself as Vanessa from South Pasadena, and poses a question for Simon: "what animal do you think you look like the most?" Simon, now surrounded by animated lemurs, responds, "I think it's a lemur. And I remember seeing a nature program once and they thought, that actually looks like me." The woman in the yellow top also appears with animated lemurs. Two women outdoors are seen as another fan asks, "Sophia, the chihuahua that you posted this morning on your Instagram story, is that yours and do you have more?" This is shown alongside a split-screen featuring chihuahuas 'Baguette' and 'Bubbles', and a photo of Sofia Vergara holding a chihuahua. Sofia Vergara's voice is heard explaining, "I have two chihuahuas in my life. One is my son, and the other one was supposed to be my dog but she hates me and loves my husband, and she's very bad dog," as Simon, with a chihuahua head superimposed on his face, laughs.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, slightly whimsical background music as Heidi, a female judge in a blue satin blouse, and the woman in a sparkly top, with pinkish-brown hair, are seated backstage against a glittering backdrop. Heidi reads a tweet in a clear, playful voice, "Hey Heidi, your hand binoculars are ridiculous. Stop it," prompting her to humorously mimic binoculars with her hands, which the woman in sparkly top joins in on, both laughing. Heidi defensively explains, "You don't understand. They're not binoculars," while the woman in sparkly top interjects, "Yes, they are!" Heidi passionately defends her need for them to see and "judge!" The scene cuts to the woman in sparkly top, who, with an amused and theatrical voice, reads another tweet: "Is Beyoncé always on this? It's Tyra Banks," reacting with amusement and a playful gesture, even mimicking a character saying, "Blue Ivy, Blue Ivy, sit down right now. Mama got to host this show, Blue." Audience laughter and music swell. The video then shifts to Simon, in a black shirt, and the bald man in sunglasses, in a dark suit, as a deep male narrator's voice reads a tweet: "AGT made Simon Cowell so soft." Simon reacts with a deadpan expression, while the bald man in sunglasses laughs, saying "Oh, good" in an amused, deep voice. Audience cheers erupt. The narrator continues, "I want Simon the savage back. The one who crushed dreams and made people cry for fun." Simon maintains his serious demeanor through quick cuts to him on stage with a microphone and back at the judges' table holding a phone, while the bald man in sunglasses laughs loudly, saying "Off." The final shot shows Simon looking serious and the bald man in sunglasses laughing at the judging panel, with red Sonic-branded cups, as the tweet about Simon’s softened persona remains on screen, concluding with the narrator's dramatic voice stating, "Password is magic."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a lively judging panel, likely from America's Got Talent, where a female judge in a sparkly top playfully asks, "Did you just look?" and "Are you guys talking?" to the bald man in sunglasses and Simon, who both deny it amidst lighthearted banter. On-screen graphics display 'MAGIC' in a blue hexagon, which is immediately confirmed by an enthusiastic host shouting, "Magic! Yeah! Magic is the correct word!" This ignites loud audience cheering and a playful, accusatory exchange among the judges about "cheating," with Simon and the bald man in sunglasses (later identified as Howie) engaging in a spirited debate with the female judges, who react with exclamations and laughter. The host then announces, "Alright, this is the final clue. We are actually tied at the moment and we are going to start with Howie," building tension. The host dramatically reveals, "The password is... Million... Dollars." After a brief pause, Howie confidently states, "Million," prompting an explosive "Oh!" from the audience and the host's declaration, "He's right! That is the word!" The audience erupts in loud cheers and applause as Howie, in his white jacket with black stripes, stands up, laughing and gesturing. Simon, in a white shirt, joins him, clapping and pointing. The woman in a pink dress stands and playfully touches Howie's head, while the woman in the sparkly top laughs, exclaiming, "I wanted to say 'Million'!" Howie and Simon then share a high-five, a handshake, and an embrace, smiling broadly as the host concludes, "Simon and Howie have won! Thank you for playing Password!" Dynamic blue and purple stage lighting illuminates the celebrating judges and the continuously cheering and clapping audience, with red Sonic-branded cups and black buzzers visible on the judges' table.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a vibrant game show setting, featuring a stage adorned with rich red curtains, golden tassels, and hanging Edison-style light bulbs that cast a warm glow, all set to upbeat, celebratory game show music and crowd cheering. A dynamic host in a light blue suit stands center stage, animatedly engaging with two seated male guests in director’s chairs, one a bald man in a black suit with glasses, and the other in a dark blue shirt. The host's excited voice exclaims "million dollars!" and "You won!" amidst the cheers, asking "Where's the check?" The scene then shifts to two female guests seated in similar chairs: one in a shimmering orange sequined dress and the other in a gray strapless gown with large silver earrings. The host, now in a calmer, instructional tone, explains a mind-reading game, asking "what word do you think he's thinking of... and vice versa, what do you think Howie is thinking of?" He instructs them to "clear your minds" and "read her mind" before counting down "Three, two, one!" As on-screen text clues like 'SHOES' and 'EARRINGS' appear, the women react with playful excitement, one adjusting her hair, the other laughing, and a female voice shouts "Yes!" The host continues, urging them to "Look at each other deeply in the eyes," before another countdown leads to a female voice excitedly shouting "Necklace!" as the corresponding clue appears. The game builds to a climax with the host's final countdown, prompting a very excited female voice to scream "Lipstick!" as the clue appears, triggering a burst of intense, celebratory music, loud crowd cheers, and the women raising their arms in celebration. The host, equally thrilled, shouts "This is freaky! Lipstick!" before concluding with a dramatic "Yield the power," maintaining the lively and theatrical game-show energy throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a theatrical stage setting, featuring rich red curtains with golden tassels and hanging Edison-style light bulbs, creating a warm, vintage ambiance. A game show buzzer sounds as the host in a light blue suit stands center stage, animatedly gesturing between two seated guests: the bald man in black suit and the man in dark blue shirt. The host audibly counts down "Three, two, one," followed by a game sound effect and his instruction to put "Hands up!" The bald man in black suit exclaims "What?!" and questions if the host said "Handsome?" The man in dark blue shirt interjects, "That was not, by the way," with the bald man in black suit adding, "This is crazy!" and "I didn't plan that," while the man in dark blue shirt insists, "I swear on my life, that was not prepped." The scene then transitions to a different setting with a vibrant 'America's Got Talent' neon backdrop, where the bald man in black suit and the man in dark blue shirt are now seated in front of the iconic logo, participating in a quiz segment. A female voice, likely from the woman in blue sequined top, asks the first on-screen question: 'I studied what profession for three years?' The man in dark blue shirt confidently states, "I know what it was," and the bald man in black suit answers "Dentistry," explaining, "Because she has amazing teeth. She's gonna be a dentist." The man in dark blue shirt adds a humorous anecdote, "She will actually floss my teeth," leading the bald man in black suit to joke about Sofia Vergara being his dentist. The woman in gray strapless gown audibly reacts, "That sounds so sexy." Another game show sound effect plays as the female voice asks the next question: 'How many Emmys have I been nominated for?' The bald man in black suit guesses "seven," while the man in dark blue shirt suggests "Two Emmys." The woman in white jacket then chimes in, "Seven times. Let's just go for the seven." After another game sound, the man in dark blue shirt asks, "Why are you looking at me like that?" and the bald man in black suit admits, "I'm guessing, I don't know." The female voice reveals the answer "Four," prompting the man in dark blue shirt to protest, "You didn't let me finish," explaining he was going to say "two times two." The bald man in black suit concludes, "Well, she should have got more." The video concludes with a still image of a woman in a white strapless gown posing on the Emmys red carpet, with the NBC and Emmys logos visible in the background, connecting to the quiz question.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with energetic, upbeat intro music and audience cheers as a close-up shows Howie Mandel, the bald man in sunglasses, animatedly speaking and gesturing with both hands. He is seated at the judging panel, wearing a black jacket over a black shirt, with a red Sonic-branded cup and a microphone in front of him. Howie declares, "The competition was brutal," and reflects on "the highest of highs and then some incredible for me, personal disappointments" from last night, adding, "I am brutally honest. That's why it was brutal. Good luck to everyone." The camera pulls back to reveal the full judging panel: Howie, the woman in sparkly top, Sofia Vergara (the woman in pink dress), and Simon Cowell, all seated behind a sleek white desk with red Sonic-branded cups and microphones. Sofia, leaning forward and smiling into her microphone, playfully interjects with an accent, "But you sometimes are also grumpy." Howie quickly retorts, "I'm not grumpy," then clarifies, "I'm translating. She said grumpy," eliciting laughter from the audience. Simon, in a dark t-shirt, then speaks, stating, "Well, you know, this is a good sign." Terry Crews, the bald host in a shiny gray suit, stands on stage holding a microphone, addressing the judges and audience, asking Sofia, "a lot of the judges, you guys disagreed on a lot of the acts last night. Why do you think that was?" Sofia responds, "first of all, we have different taste," to which Howie immediately interjects, "We all have the same taste." Sofia continues, laughing, "Some of them don't know what they what they what they're supposed to know," prompting Howie to assert, "I know everything we know." Sofia then playfully describes Howie as "grumpy sometimes and Simon is sometimes loco," to which Simon, with expressive hand gestures, places a hand on his chest and admits, "I admit it," as the audience cheers and laughs in the dimly lit arena with blue and purple stage lighting, creating a vibrant, live show atmosphere. The NBC 'LIVE' logo is displayed in the bottom left corner throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The America's Got Talent stage glows with dynamic blue and purple lighting as the audience cheers and claps. The host, a woman with voluminous curly blonde hair in a black lace dress, stands center stage, welcoming everyone to "Buzzer Buddy!" She then playfully asks Howie, the bald man in sunglasses, what he'd discard first from Simon's closet. Howie, speaking animatedly, begins to respond, but Mel B, the woman in the yellow top, quickly interjects, highlighting Simon's "very minimal choices" as "he wears the same thing." Howie agrees, and Mel B specifies "the white t-shirt and the white dress shirt," or "the bell-bottom pants." Howie confirms "the white t-shirt," and Mel B exclaims, "The white Simon everything!" Howie concurs, "That is everything." Mel B then declares, "I'm gonna give you that point," as the score graphic updates to '2 2' with Simon raising his hand. She asks the cheering audience if they agree, concluding that's "pretty much all that Simon wears." Next, Heidi, the woman in the sparkly top, enthusiastically praises the young man in the black shirt with a cross necklace, declaring he "did so much magic" and made her "believe in magic," calling it "amazing" and "better than your audition." She then eagerly asks, "And I really wanna know what Simon thinks!" The audience chants "Simon!" as Heidi presses, "Did he win you over?" Simon, in a dark t-shirt, calmly replies, "I think I helped you." Heidi playfully disagrees with a "No!" but Simon insists, "I do. I really, really do. I think..." as the segment concludes.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Simon, a male judge in a dark t-shirt, speaking expressively with his hand on his chest, his voice acknowledging a performer's improvement: "by me saying no at the auditions, I think has pushed you to be a better magician." He then humorously declares, "I am officially an idiot," eliciting audience cheers. The camera briefly cuts to the young male performer, a magician in a gray collared shirt and cross necklace, smiling confidently on stage under purple lighting. Simon, now in a white shirt, continues his animated reaction, gesturing with both hands into the microphone. The full judging panel, including the bald man in sunglasses (Howie), the woman in a sparkly top, and the woman in a pink dress, react with smiles and engagement as Simon thoughtfully touches his chin. The host, Terry, a bald man in a shiny dark blue suit, takes center stage with a microphone, gesturing expansively. His energetic voice addresses Howie, expressing heartfelt tribute: "what you have done for the show and as a friend, as one of the most amazing people I've ever worked with. I wanted to pay you a tribute to show your significance and most importantly, your importance for the 10 years you've been on the show." Simon then hands over to Terry, saying, "Over to you, Terry," followed by more audience cheers. Terry announces, "It's out on Sunset Boulevard, okay? Let's take a look. Here we go! Count it down with me, people!" as the camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the America's Got Talent stage. A large screen displays a cityscape and a countdown from 04 to 02, then transitions to a billboard reading 'Happy 10 YEAR ANNIVERSARY Howie!' with Howie's photo, the AGT logo, and 'TUES & WED NBC'. Howie's voice is heard asking, "Where am I looking?" before Terry leads the audience in a loud countdown: "Five! Four! Three! Two! One!" The dynamic lighting shifts from deep purples and blues to warm stage lights, emphasizing the celebratory and emotional tone of the moment.
